title: Understanding Gravity's Effect
text: On the Moon, the gravitational acceleration is one-sixth of that on Earth. This means that the athlete's jump will be affected by this change in gravity.

title: Relating Jump Distance to Gravity
text: The distance an athlete can jump is inversely proportional to the gravitational acceleration. If gravity decreases, the jump distance increases.

title: Calculating the Jump Distance on the Moon
text: If the athlete can jump $$8.0$$ $$m$$ on Earth, and the Moon's gravity is one-sixth of Earth's, calculate the jump distance on the Moon using the formula: $$distance_{on,moon}=distance_{on,earth} \frac{earth_{gravity}}{moon_{gravity}}$$.
